learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate developing intermediate-level performance skills on one continuing instrument and functional beginner-level skills on a second instrument.
Design and implement effective instructional approaches aligned with contemporary pedagogical approaches and the Education Queensland Instrumental Music Curriculum.
Identify technical and musical issues in beginner performers and propose appropriate pedagogical interventions.
Critically reflect on personal teaching practice and justify pedagogical decisions.

assessments.task_description: In this task, you will demonstrate your progressing performance skills on the two instruments you are studying this semester. The required technical work and solo repertoire will be assigned by your instrument tutor during class. Feedback from this task should be used to inform future personal practice in this course.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit one video per instrument via EchoVideo in Blackboard
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
21/09/2026 - 25/09/2026

The exam will occur during the Week 9 lecture.
assessments.task_description:
In this task, you will demonstrate your understanding of pedagogical approaches and teaching strategies, as well as your ability to identify technical and musical issues in beginner playing and design resources to address common challenges. You will be asked to explain and justify your choices. 

Allowed materials: Education Queensland Instrumental Music Curriculum (clean hardcopy), Pencils, Pens, Eraser, Sharpener, Highlighter

Provided materials: Exam paper, Manuscript paper, Plain paper for notes
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description: In this task, you will plan and teach one 20min lesson with a mixed instrument group of players from our class who will be playing one of the instruments they are studying as part of this course. Groups will be assigned by the tutor in your specialisation area. The teaching will happen outside of class, at a mutually agreed-upon time and location. You will record your teaching, then annotate it using EchoVideo to identify and justify the teaching strategies used, analyse their effectiveness, and cite learner outcomes and other observations. Clear alignment to an appropriate Education Queensland Instrumental Music Curriculum level should be evident.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: In this task, you will demonstrate your performance skills on the two instruments you have studied this semester. Your performance on Instrument A should demonstrate skills aligned with the Education Queensland Instrumental Music Curriculum Level 4, while your performance on Instrument B should align with Level 2. Your performance may include scales, technical exercises and solo repertoire, and may be sourced from the class method book or other appropriate sources (copies of other music should be provided). You are required to justify your selection of each scale/exercise/piece in your video prior to playing, aligning it to the appropriate curriculum level. Ensemble skills will be assessed via a self-recording taken in class during Week 13.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit one video per instrument via EchoVideo in Blackboard
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Tutorials

You will attend either the Strings tutorial or the Wind/Brass/Percussion tutorial each week depending on your chosen specialisation.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Tutorials You will attend either the Strings tutorial or the Wind/Brass/Percussion tutorial each week depending on your chosen specialisation.

learning_activities.topic:
Lectures

A one hour lecture per week for all students enrolled in the course.

Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04
